name: dompurify-expert
description: DOMPurify architectural opinions and decision frameworks

opinions.title: When to Sanitize HTML (Storage vs Render)
opinions.description: Whether to sanitize HTML before storage, at render time, or both
opinions.rationale: Sanitizing at both storage and render provides defense in depth. Storage-time sanitization protects against rule changes. Render-time sanitization is final safeguard. Trade-off: performance vs security

options.name: Sanitize at Both Storage and Render (Recommended)
options.description: Sanitize HTML before storing in database AND before rendering
pros: Old data remains safe if rules tightened

options.name: Sanitize Only at Render
options.description: Store unsanitized HTML, sanitize when rendering
pros: Can adjust rules without re-sanitizing storage
cons: Old data vulnerable if rules loosened then tightened

options.name: Sanitize Only at Storage
options.description: Sanitize before storage, trust stored content
cons: Cannot tighten rules for old content
Must re-sanitize entire database if rules change

opinions.title: Centralized vs Decentralized Sanitization
opinions.description: Whether to centralize sanitization in @company/security or allow per-app configuration
opinions.rationale: Centralized sanitization in @company/security ensures consistency, easier auditing, and single source of truth. Prevents developers from using unsafe configurations

options.name: Centralized in @company/security (Recommended)
options.description: All sanitization logic and configs in private NPM package
cons: Must update package for rule changes

options.name: Decentralized (Per-App)
options.description: Each app maintains own DOMPurify configurations
cons: Each app can make unsafe choices
